top_5.title: Facility as Data Cooperative
top_5.evidence_summary: Don't sell TO facilities—make facilities co-owners of the data. Revenue share on all brand/media/coaching sales, but facilities get voting rights on what data gets sold and to whom. This converts Courtana from vendor to partner, makes data collection faster (facilities improve their own systems), and creates alignment where facilities actively promote the ecosystem.

follow_up.follow_up_question: What's the smallest experiment we could run to test this idea? What would success look like in 30 days?

top_5.title: Brand Athlete Passport
top_5.evidence_summary: Flip the model: instead of detecting brands on players, brands PAY to issue digital credentials to players wearing their gear. Players get badges (Courtana integration), brands get first-party wear data, and players become walking billboards who voluntarily scan their own equipment. This turns detection into opt-in attestation—brands trust the data because players verified it themselves.

follow_up.follow_up_question: What's the smallest experiment we could run to test this idea? What would success look like in 30 days?

top_5.title: Personal AI Coach (Freemium → Brand Sponsorship)
top_5.evidence_summary: Give every player a free AI coach that analyzes their Courtana clips in real-time, suggests drills, and tracks improvement. It's freemium. But: the coach recommends equipment brands based on YOUR shot patterns—if you slice backhand, it suggests a specific JOOLA paddle. Brands pay to be recommended. Users get free coaching, brands get conversion data, and you own the recommendation layer.

follow_up.follow_up_question: What's the smallest experiment we could run to test this idea? What would success look like in 30 days?

top_5.title: Tournament-as-a-Service Powered by Video
top_5.evidence_summary: Use the clip analysis to run fully-automated remote tournaments where judges/commentary are AI, bracket logic is self-adjusting based on live performance ratings, and real-time stats are displayed on courts. Sell this SaaS to leagues and facilities. Each tournament generates a huge dataset, every player's improvement is mapped, and brands sponsor brackets. Makes the data collection itself the product.

follow_up.follow_up_question: What's the smallest experiment we could run to test this idea? What would success look like in 30 days?

top_5.title: Viral Clip Futures Market
top_5.evidence_summary: Launch a prediction market where users bet on which clips will go viral 24-48 hours before they trend. Settle bets using actual social metrics. The wisdom-of-crowds forecast signal becomes a content ranking algorithm, which gets licensed back to social platforms. More players → better predictions → more licensing → more players.

follow_up.follow_up_question: What's the smallest experiment we could run to test this idea? What would success look like in 30 days?

top_20.title: Facility as Data Cooperative
top_20.evidence_summary: Don't sell TO facilities—make facilities co-owners of the data. Revenue share on all brand/media/coaching sales, but facilities get voting rights on what data gets sold and to whom. This converts Courtana from vendor to partner, makes data collection faster (facilities improve their own systems), and creates alignment where facilities actively promote the ecosystem.
top_20.recommended_action: Evaluate: network_effect play

top_20.title: Brand Athlete Passport
top_20.evidence_summary: Flip the model: instead of detecting brands on players, brands PAY to issue digital credentials to players wearing their gear. Players get badges (Courtana integration), brands get first-party wear data, and players become walking billboards who voluntarily scan their own equipment. This turns detection into opt-in attestation—brands trust the data because players verified it themselves.
top_20.recommended_action: Evaluate: crowdsource play

top_20.title: Personal AI Coach (Freemium → Brand Sponsorship)
top_20.evidence_summary: Give every player a free AI coach that analyzes their Courtana clips in real-time, suggests drills, and tracks improvement. It's freemium. But: the coach recommends equipment brands based on YOUR shot patterns—if you slice backhand, it suggests a specific JOOLA paddle. Brands pay to be recommended. Users get free coaching, brands get conversion data, and you own the recommendation layer.
top_20.recommended_action: Evaluate: b2c_product play

top_20.title: Tournament-as-a-Service Powered by Video
top_20.evidence_summary: Use the clip analysis to run fully-automated remote tournaments where judges/commentary are AI, bracket logic is self-adjusting based on live performance ratings, and real-time stats are displayed on courts. Sell this SaaS to leagues and facilities. Each tournament generates a huge dataset, every player's improvement is mapped, and brands sponsor brackets. Makes the data collection itself the product.
top_20.recommended_action: Evaluate: platform_play play

top_20.title: Viral Clip Futures Market
top_20.evidence_summary: Launch a prediction market where users bet on which clips will go viral 24-48 hours before they trend. Settle bets using actual social metrics. The wisdom-of-crowds forecast signal becomes a content ranking algorithm, which gets licensed back to social platforms. More players → better predictions → more licensing → more players.
top_20.recommended_action: Evaluate: monetization_novel play

top_20.title: Equipment Trade-In Registry Backed by Video History
top_20.evidence_summary: Create a secondary market for used pickleball gear where every paddle/racket's Courtana video history is embedded as proof of performance. A JOOLA paddle with 200 competitive clips showing 72% win rate commands a premium. Brands underwrite this market (buy-back guarantees), players earn by selling used gear with provenance, and you monetize every transaction plus the certified video escrow service.
top_20.recommended_action: Evaluate: creator_economy play

top_20.title: Equipment Performance Insurance
top_20.evidence_summary: Partner with brands to offer 'performance guarantees' backed by video evidence. A player buys a JOOLA paddle with DaaS-verified performance insurance: if your shot success rate drops >15% after 30 days, get a refund or replacement. Brands get behavioral data, players get risk-free buying, and the video dataset becomes the underwriting engine. Makes equipment a data-generating asset.
top_20.recommended_action: Evaluate: partnership_flip play

top_20.title: Pickleball Player Stock Exchange
top_20.evidence_summary: Create tradeable synthetic equity in rising players' 'brand value' based on Courtana clips, viral scores, and badge progression. Fans, sponsors, and micro-investors buy/sell player shares. As players improve (detected via video), their equity rises. Drives obsessive self-improvement loops and creates a speculative market that funds player development—brands sponsor based on equity price, not sponsorship deals.
top_20.recommended_action: Evaluate: platform_play play

top_20.title: Sponsorship whitespace detected in 716 clips (84.9%) — sell to brands
top_20.evidence_summary: We detect unbranded equipment/space in 716 clips. This is the gap between what's visible and what's branded — sellable data.
top_20.recommended_action: Package sponsorship whitespace data as 'brand opportunity report' for equipment companies

source_clips.summary: A 17-shot driving rally ends with an unforced error into the net.
Players engage in a prolonged kitchen dink rally, demonstrating patience and control.
Players engage in a consistent rally of dinks at the kitchen line.

top_20.title: Endorsement intelligence: 75 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences
top_20.evidence_summary: Top pairs: PickleBill-JOOLA(284x), Chintan-JOOLA(257x), Ironvarr-JOOLA(175x), Tmf-JOOLA(143x), Santinofuntila-JOOLA(136x)
top_20.recommended_action: Build 'brand ambassador identification' product for equipment companies

top_20.title: Longitudinal tracking possible: 34 players with 5+ clips
top_20.evidence_summary: 34 players have enough data for improvement tracking. Top: PickleBill(161), Chintan(155), Ironvarr(81), Santinofuntila(67), Tmf(59)
top_20.recommended_action: Build player improvement trajectory product — track skill changes over time

top_20.title: Viral arc: 'top_play' is 130.0x more likely in top clips (13.0% vs 0.0%)
top_20.evidence_summary: Story arc 'top_play' appears in 13.0% of top-viral clips but only 0.0% of bottom-viral clips.
top_20.recommended_action: Prioritize 'top_play' clips in automated highlight reels

top_20.title: Brand-skill split: PICKLEBALL KINGDOM users excel at kitchen (7.5), HEAD users at power (4.88)
top_20.evidence_summary: Among brands with 10+ clips: PICKLEBALL KINGDOM has highest avg kitchen mastery (7.5), while HEAD has highest power rating (4.88).
top_20.recommended_action: Build brand-skill correlation report for brand partnerships

top_20.title: Viral formula: quality (7.59 vs 5.42) + watchability (6.45 vs 3.14)
top_20.evidence_summary: Top 100 viral clips: quality=7.59, watchability=6.45. Bottom 100: quality=5.42, watchability=3.14. Both metrics track strongly with viral potential.
top_20.recommended_action: Use (quality + watchability) / 2 as quick viral prediction score

top_20.title: Worst predicted badges: serve returner (F1=0.875), hustler (F1=0.769)
top_20.evidence_summary: Lowest F1 badges: serve returner (F1=0.875), hustler (F1=0.769). These need prompt improvement.
top_20.recommended_action: Add specific examples for these badge types in Gemini prompt

top_20.title: Social caption generation at 99.8% — ready to productize
top_20.evidence_summary: Social media captions are generated for 841/843 clips. This is already a viable content-as-a-service product.
top_20.recommended_action: Create API endpoint for social caption generation — charge per clip

top_20.title: Viral shot: 'volley' clips average 4.46 viral vs 'serve' at 1.25
top_20.evidence_summary: Shot viral averages: volley=4.46, dink=3.37, drive=3.31, serve=1.25
top_20.recommended_action: Prioritize highlight reels featuring high-viral shot types

top_20.title: DaaS badge prediction: F1=0.97 (precision=0.98, recall=0.96)
top_20.evidence_summary: Across 287 verified clips: 999 correct predictions, 16 false positives, 41 missed badges. Overall F1=0.972.
top_20.recommended_action: Target recall improvement: add more badge examples to Gemini prompt

top_20.title: Signature moves = viral gold: 813 clips with signatures avg 3.4 viral vs 3.9
top_20.evidence_summary: 813 clips have identified signature moves, averaging 3.4 viral vs 3.9 for clips without.
top_20.recommended_action: Auto-tag signature move clips as premium content

source_clips.summary: A 17-shot driving rally ends with an unforced error into the net.
Players engage in a prolonged kitchen dink rally, demonstrating patience and control.
Players engage in a consistent rally of dinks at the kitchen line.

top_20.title: Coaching feedback covers 99.8% of clips — drill library is buildable now
top_20.evidence_summary: Coaching analysis available for 841/843 clips.
top_20.recommended_action: Build 'AI Coaching Library' — categorized drills from real game footage

all_findings.title: Top content creators: PickleBill leads with 161 clips
all_findings.evidence_summary:
PickleBill: 161 clips, viral=3.4, Steady Eddie
Chintan: 155 clips, viral=3.4, Dink Specialist
Ironvarr: 81 clips, viral=3.4, Kitchen Master
Santinofuntila: 67 clips, viral=4.0, Dink Specialist
Tmf: 59 clips, viral=3.6, Kitchen Master
all_findings.recommended_action: Create player spotlight feature for top contributors

source_clips.summary: Players engage in a consistent rally of dinks at the kitchen line.
Two players engage in a consistent dink rally at the kitchen line.
Two players engage in a consistent, extended dink rally at the kitchen line.

all_findings.title: Player archetype distribution: Kitchen Master dominates (20/38 players)
all_findings.evidence_summary: Among 38 identified players: Kitchen Master: 20, Steady Eddie: 9, All-Rounder: 5, Dink Specialist: 4
all_findings.recommended_action: Build player DNA product with archetype classification as key feature

all_findings.title: Skill standout: Ajr averages 8.5 in consistency across 6 clips
all_findings.evidence_summary: Ajr skill profile: consistency=8.5, kitchen=8.2, touch=8.0, composure=7.5, court_iq=7.0, court_coverage=6.2, athleticism=5.2, power=3.0, creativity=3.0
all_findings.recommended_action: Feature Ajr in coaching product showcase

source_clips.summary: A long kitchen rally ends with a powerful overhead smash.
A lengthy kitchen rally featuring consistent dinks ends with a net error.
Players engage in a lengthy kitchen rally, ending with a net error.

all_findings.title: Rally length matters: long rallies are more viral (long=3.6 vs short=2.3)
all_findings.evidence_summary: Avg rally: 16.9 shots. Long rallies (15+): 485 clips, avg viral 3.6. Short (4-): 21 clips, avg viral 2.3.
all_findings.recommended_action: Use rally length as a quality signal in highlight curation

all_findings.title: Tier accuracy gap: 'wood' at 100% vs 'bronze' at 94%
all_findings.evidence_summary: Badge tier prediction accuracy: wood=100%, brick=97%, silver=97%, gold=94%, bronze=94%
all_findings.recommended_action: Focus prompt improvement on bronze-tier badges

all_findings.title: 'strategic' is 77.0% dink — coaching archetype
all_findings.evidence_summary: The 'strategic' arc (583 clips) is dominated by dink shots (77.0%). This is a clear coaching pattern.
all_findings.recommended_action: Create 'strategic' coaching module focused on dink technique

all_findings.title: Emerging brands detected: 13 brands with 2-8 appearances
all_findings.evidence_summary: Rare but real brand detections: PBS (5), adidas (4), Foscam (4), PKLR (3), H2 (3), USA (3), Nicol (3), Life Time (2). These represent market entrants or niche brands.
all_findings.recommended_action: Monitor emerging brand frequency over next 500 clips for trend confirmation

all_findings.title: 'dink' players skew kitchen: power=3.0 vs kitchen=7.6
all_findings.evidence_summary: Players using dink as dominant shot (585 clips) average power=3.0 and kitchen=7.6.
all_findings.recommended_action: Use shot type as player skill segmentation signal

all_findings.title: Viral skill bias: kitchen = 7.5 in viral vs 5.6 in non-viral clips
all_findings.evidence_summary: Viral clips show kitchen average of 7.5 vs 5.6 in low-viral clips.
all_findings.recommended_action: Consider kitchen as a viral prediction feature

all_findings.title: Best predicted badges: spike! (F1=1.0), spin doctor (F1=1.0), bronze level up (F1=1.0)
all_findings.evidence_summary: Top performing badges by F1 score: spike! (F1=1.0), spin doctor (F1=1.0), bronze level up (F1=1.0)
all_findings.recommended_action: Use these badges as confidence anchors for the prediction system

all_findings.title: Viral rally length: top clips avg 17 shots vs bottom 13
all_findings.evidence_summary: Top-viral clips average 17.1 shots per rally, bottom-viral average 13.1.
all_findings.recommended_action: Investigate optimal rally length range for highlight reels
